question: what was the key factor that allowed hershey and chase
ground_truth: The results of the Avery–MacLeod–McCarty experiment
answer: The key factor that allowed Hershey and Chase to determine that DNA, not protein, is the genetic material was their observation that most of the protein did not enter the host bacterial cell when bacteriophages infected bacteria, while almost all the radioactive S (indicating protein) remained outside the cell. This suggested that it was the DNA, not the protein, that entered the cell and played a role in producing progeny inside a bacterium.
